title: Media Composition for Diplonema papillatum culture
steps: Dissolve the sea salt and tryptone in distilled water. For 500ml 20g of sea salts 5g of tryptone 495ml of distilled water
Filter it through Whatman filter paper to remove undissolved salt
Mix it with magnetic stirrer and filter the media again with (0.22µm) filter

Place the media at 27°C for dense culture or 15°C for optimal growth. It is also recommended that the media should be tested for the presence of any contaminants, prior to the addition of cells, by keeping overnight incubation of media in the particular incubator
After half an hour incubation of media in the incubator, add healthy cell culture to the flask 1:10 times diluted
Check the general appearance of the cells immediately after adding the cells in the media and also after 6-8 h of incubation in the particular media using inverted phase contrast microscope.
creator: Binnypreet Kaur
